pendingTriage.author: CJS
pendingTriage.contentPreview: sti, I was notified that SpotOn v2.3.1 is available. But this version won't install, since apparently there is a checksum mismatch:   	Code: 	[26-07-02 18:11:38.5302] Slim::Utils::PluginDownloader::_installDownload (218) digest does not match /var/lib/squeezeboxserver/cache/DownloadedPlugins/SpotOn.
pendingTriage.draft:
CJS, thanks for the report — this is a packaging issue on my end, not a problem with your installation.

What's happened here is that the [B]repository ZIP and the published checksum in the plugin manifest are out of sync[/B]. This typically happens when a release ZIP gets rebuilt or re-uploaded after the manifest SHA1 was already published (e.g. a hotfix to the archive without bumping the version string).

[B]What the log means:[/B]
[CODE]
expected 9cf94b5d8e9077da93dafc651698a7a790a04a38
got     5ca9514c560a72fa6755de8b6dc2bf839c0572cd
[/CODE]
LMS downloaded the ZIP fine, but the SHA1 of what arrived doesn't match what the manifest declared — so the installer correctly refuses to proceed. Your system is working as intended; the fault is in the release.

[B]Short-term workaround:[/B] You can install manually by downloading the ZIP directly from the GitHub release page and installing via [B]LMS Settings → Plugins → Install from ZIP[/B]. Note this bypasses checksum verification, so only do it from the official GitHub release:
[URL]https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/releases[/URL]

[B]I'll push a corrected manifest[/B] with the right SHA1 as soon as I've confirmed the ZIP is stable. Once that's live, the normal auto-install path should work again — you may need to clear [CODE]/var/lib/squeezeboxserver/cache/DownloadedPlugins/SpotOn.zip[/CODE] first so LMS re-fetches cleanly.

Sorry for the inconvenience — I've opened a tracking note internally. If others hit this in the meantime, please reply here so I can gauge how widely the bad manifest has propagated.

For future reference, packaging issues like this are best logged at:
[URL]https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/issues[/URL]

But this version won't install, since apparently there
pendingTriage.draft:
[QUOTE=urknall;134]thats already fixed 2.3.2 is out[/QUOTE]

Thanks for the quick heads-up, urknall! CJS — what urknall said. The checksum mismatch in v2.3.1 was caused by a release artifact issue on my end; [B]v2.3.2[/B] is already out and corrects it.

If the update still doesn't appear automatically, try the following:

[LIST=1]
[*]Go to [B]LMS Settings → Plugins[/B] and click [B]Check for Updates[/B]
[*]If v2.3.2 still doesn't show, clear the plugin download cache manually and retry — LMS may have cached the broken zip. The stale file would be at something like:[CODE]/var/lib/squeezeboxserver/cache/DownloadedPlugins/SpotOn.zip[/CODE]Delete it, then trigger the update check again.
[/LIST]

Sorry for the inconvenience — the v2.3.1 artifact should never have gone out in that state. If you're still hitting issues after trying v2.3.2, please let me know here or open a ticket at [URL]https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/issues[/URL] with your LMS log snippet.

pendingTriage.author: Andy_NO
pendingTriage.contentPreview: There is something odd with the plugin. I have it installed and can access it on my players but I cannot see it on LMS web page at the active plugins. The server also constantly demands reboot.
pendingTriage.draft:
Hi Andy_NO, welcome to the thread!

These two symptoms together — plugin not visible in the active plugins list and LMS repeatedly requesting a reboot — are a bit unusual and could have a few different causes.

[B]Most likely: a failed or partial installation[/B]

Looking at the post just above yours from CJS (#133), there's an active checksum mismatch issue with v2.3.1 that's preventing clean installation. This could explain both symptoms — LMS may have partially staged the plugin but not fully activated it, leaving it in a state where it keeps asking for a restart that never fully resolves things.

[B]Things to check:[/B]

[LIST=1]
[*]After the reboot prompt, are you actually restarting LMS (not just the web UI)? A full server restart is required, not just a page refresh.
[*]Check your LMS log for any errors around the SpotOn plugin — look for lines containing [CODE]SpotOn[/CODE] or [CODE]digest does not match[/CODE] around the time of installation.
[*]Try removing SpotOn via [B]Settings → Plugins[/B], restarting LMS, then reinstalling. If you hit the checksum error CJS reported, that's the culprit.
[/LIST]

[B]To share your log snippet:[/B] Go to [B]Settings → Advanced → Logging[/B] and check the server log, or use [B]SpotOn Settings → Diagnostics[/B] if you can reach that page at all.

Could you confirm:
[LIST]
[*]Which LMS version you're running?
[*]Which SpotOn version was offered for install?
[*]Do you see any [CODE]digest does not match[/CODE] lines in your log?
[/LIST]

If this is indeed the checksum issue, please also add a note to the GitHub issue tracker so it gets tracked: [URL]https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/issues[/URL]

pendingTriage.author: CJS

I have it installed and can access it on my players but I cannot
pendingTriage.draft:
[QUOTE=CJS;136]There was a problem with installation of version 2.3.1. Now version 2.3.2 is out and in my case that version installed fine.[/QUOTE]

Thanks CJS for jumping in and helping out — much appreciated!

To confirm: the checksum mismatch in v2.3.1 was caused by a release artifact issue on my end. It's been resolved, and [B]v2.3.3[/B] is the current stable version. Sorry for the rapid-fire releases — a few things needed quick follow-up fixes after the OGG passthrough changes landed.

[B]@Andy_NO[/B] — the symptoms you're describing (plugin accessible on players but not visible in the Active Plugins list, persistent reboot prompts) are consistent with a partially failed installation. This can happen if a bad zip was cached locally before the fix. Here's what to try:

[B]1. Clear the plugin download cache:[/B]
[CODE]rm /var/lib/squeezeboxserver/cache/DownloadedPlugins/SpotOn.zip[/CODE]

[B]2. Restart LMS, then reinstall SpotOn from Plugin Manager.[/B]

[B]3. Check that you end up on v2.3.3[/B] — you should see it listed under [B]My Apps → SpotOn[/B] after a clean install and server restart.

If the reboot loop persists after a clean install, please grab a log snippet around the server startup showing any SpotOn-related errors and post it here, or open a GitHub issue with the details:

[URL]https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/issues[/URL]

You can also check [B]SpotOn Settings → Diagnostics[/B] for a diagnostic bundle once the plugin is properly showing up. Happy to help track it down from there.

pendingTriage.author: plasticator2
pendingTriage.contentPreview: Hi, first off can I say that SpotOn looks really promising - thanks so much! Loads easily with Spotify Connect. One major problem, is that playback either through Spotify Connect or Material Skin is much louder - and distorted - than with Spotty. Yesterday I reinstalled and it sounded fine but that
pendingTriage.draft:
Hi plasticator2, welcome, and thanks for the kind words!

The volume/distortion difference you're describing is a known area we're tracking — see [URL=https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/issues/74]GitHub Issue #74[/URL] which covers Connect volume being higher than Browse mode on non-fixed-volume players. The underlying cause is that SpotOn and Spotty may apply different gain/normalisation defaults, and Squeezelite's own volume handling adds another variable.

A few things to check and try in the meantime:

[B]1. SpotOn volume/normalisation settings[/B]
In SpotOn Settings, look for any volume normalisation or ReplayGain options and try toggling them. Even with ReplayGain off in LMS, librespot (the Rust backend) has its own normalisation setting that can be enabled independently.

[B]2. librespot normalisation[/B]
SpotOn passes flags through to librespot. If normalisation is enabled on the librespot side, it can actually boost quiet tracks and cause clipping. Try explicitly disabling it in SpotOn's advanced/extra CLI flags field:
[CODE]--disable-normalisation[/CODE]

[B]3. Squeezelite volume[/B]
On piCorePlayer, check that Squeezelite isn't set to a fixed output volume above 100% in the pCP web interface (Tweaks → Squeezelite settings). A value like [CODE]-V 100[/CODE] is fine but some configs inadvertently go higher.

[B]4. The one-day it worked[/B]
That's interesting — could you recall if you'd changed any settings during that reinstall, or if it was a fresh config? That might help narrow it down.

I can see you've attached the diagnostic bundle — could you also paste the relevant section showing the librespot startup flags (search for [CODE]Starting librespot[/CODE] or [CODE]stream_port[/CODE] in the log)? That'll confirm exactly which flags are being passed.

If the issue persists, please open a ticket at [URL=https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/issues]GitHub Issues[/URL] with the diagnostic file attached — that'll make it much easier to track. Thanks!
